title: Misandropsis monoica
intro: Misandropsis monoica, also known as Kava, is a perennial plant native to the Pacific Island region, characterized by its woody stem and heart-shaped leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing relief from anxiety, insomnia, and other stress-related disorders due to its anxiolytic and sedative effects. The bioactive constituents of Misandropsis monoica include kavalactones, specifically kawain and demethylkawain, as well as other compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. In herbal medicine, Misandropsis monoica is commonly prepared as a standardized extract or a traditional tea, also known as kava tea, which is often used to promote relaxation and reduce muscle tension.
taxonomy: Misandropsis monoica is classified in the family Asteraceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics, such as its composite flowers and the presence of ray and disc florets. Within the Asteraceae family, Misandropsis monoica is typically placed in the tribe Inuleae, which includes a range of related species. The tribe Inuleae is further divided into several subtribes, with Misandropsis monoica likely belonging to the subtribe Inulinae. Further research may be necessary to confirm the exact classification of Misandropsis monoica within the Asteraceae family.
